MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-02-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301
ORDER DENYING MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA ASSOCIATION OF HEALTH FACILITIES' MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. PETITIONERS HAVE FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING RESPONDENT ABUSED ITS DISCRETION. THE COURT'S REVIEW IS HIGHLY DEFERENTIAL. EVEN ASSUMING THAT PETITIONERS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE THAT COULD SUPPORT A DIFFERENT RESULT THAT IS INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THAT RESPONDENT ABUSED ITS DISCRETION BY REACHING THE CONCLUSION IT DID ON THE BASIS OF THE FACTORS IT CONSIDERED. OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR # 8620 (301/PJB)

Off Calendar Aug-03-2009 Continued To Oct-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate; Declarations Of Darryl Nixon And Mark A. Johnson
Off Calendar Jul-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES- ATTY. MARK A. JOHNSON OF THE HOOPER, LUNDY & BOOKMAN, INC., TEL. NO. (619) 744-7301 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. KARIN SCHWARTZ, SUPERVISING DEPUTY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 703-1382 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA ASSOCIATION OF HEALTH FACILITIES' Motion For Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. PETITIONERS HAVE FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING RESPONDENT ABUSED ITS DISCRETION. THE COURT'S REVIEW IS HIGHLY DEFERENTIAL. EVEN ASSUMING THAT PETITIONERS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE THAT COULD SUPPORT A DIFFERENT RESULT THAT IS INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE THAT RESPONDENT ABUSED ITS DISCRETION BY REACHING THE CONCLUSION IT DID ON THE BASIS OF THE FACTORS IT CONSIDERED. OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR # 8620 = (301/PJB)

Off Calendar Jan-26-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Karin S. Schwartz, Deputy Attorney General (415) 703-1382, for Defendant and Moving Party State Department Of Health Services Of The State of California
Mark Reagan, Esq. and Mark Johnson, Esq., Hooper Lundy & Bookman (415) 875-8519, for Plaintiff and Opposing Party California Association of Health Facilities
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling: Plaintiff's request for Judicial Notice of final statement of reasons is GRANTED. DEFENDANT STATE DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint Or In The Alternative, Motion To Strike Demand For Retroactive Monetary Relief: SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the 1st - 3rd Causes of Action. Prevailing party to submit form of order. (301/PHA/DS) Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR #5220
